prompt: A MeridianMart Offline Commerce platform capable of running entirely on an internal store network provides a full shopping and fulfillment loop for Regular Shoppers, Store Staff, and Administrators. Regular Shoppers sign in with a locally validated username and password, browse a catalog, view product details, add items to favorites and cart, rate purchases on a 1–5 scale, and complete checkout at a staffed register. The Thymeleaf-rendered UI presents clear interaction feedback such as "added to cart," stock warnings when on-hand quantity falls below 2 units, and a purchase confirmation screen with a printable receipt number and transaction timestamp in 12-hour time. Shoppers see personalized "Recommended for you" items on the home page and product pages, plus in-app notifications for order status and pickup readiness; notification frequency is capped at 5 per day per shopper to prevent spam. Staff can look up transactions by receipt number, initiate refunds, and mark items as ready for pickup; administrators manage feature flags and operational thresholds and can review compliance reports. The backend uses Spring Boot to expose decoupled REST-style endpoints consumed by Thymeleaf pages, validating inputs and enforcing authorization by role before persisting to MySQL. A collaborative filtering engine records local behavior events (views, favorites, add-to-cart, purchases, and ratings) to build a user–item matrix; it computes similarity using cosine or Pearson correlation, generates Top-10 recommendations per shopper, and handles cold start by falling back to category popularity from the last 30 days and new arrivals. Recommendations are cached locally with a 60-minute TTL and refreshed by scheduled offline jobs nightly at 2:00 AM, with sparse-data safeguards requiring at least 3 shared interactions before a similarity score is used. Payments and settlement are implemented as offline WeChat Pay-presented transactions recorded from POS confirmations without calling external gateways; refunds, optional deposits/pre-authorizations, reconciliation, and failed-payment compensation are handled via idempotent transaction keys and distributed locks to prevent double posting under concurrency. Security and governance include immutable audit logs for critical actions (login failures, refunds, configuration changes), key-field change history, sensitive-data masking on screens and exports, and encrypted-at-rest storage for credentials and payment tokens using AES-256 with salted password hashing. APIs require request signing with timestamp and nonce to prevent replay within a 5-minute window, rate limiting defaults to 60 requests per minute per user, and brute-force protection locks accounts for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Backup/restore runs locally with daily full backups retained 30 days, restore traceability, and data retention rules that anonymize inactive shopper profiles after 24 months unless tied to open disputes.
